epigraph.text: For by wise guidance you can wage your war, and in abundance of counselors there is victory.
epigraph.quote: Multiple regression allows us to control for confounding variables.

content.text: Multiple regression extends simple regression to multiple predictors.

content.title: Multiple Linear Regression
content.content:
\[
 Y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 X_1 + \beta_2 X_2 + \cdots + \beta_k X_k + \epsilon
\]
Each $\beta_j$ represents the change in $Y$ for a unit change in $X_j$, 
*holding all other predictors constant*.

content.text:
Unlike simple regression, each coefficient in multiple regression is 
a **partial** effect—the effect of one predictor while controlling 
for others.

content.title: Multiple Regression
content.problem:
A model predicts house price from size and age:
\[
 \hat{\text{Price}} = 50000 + 100 \times \text{Size} - 2000 \times \text{Age}
\]
Interpretation:

- Each additional sq.\ ft.\ adds \$100 to predicted price, 
 holding age constant.
 \item Each additional year of age reduces price by \$2000, holding 
 size constant.

content.text:
Adding predictors always increases $R^2$. The **adjusted $R^2$** 
penalizes for the number of predictors:
\[
 R^2_{adj} = 1 - \frac{(1-R^2)(n-1)}{n-k-1}
\]
When predictors are highly correlated with each other:
